service_character.name: Trust Scoring
service_character.role: Evidence-based reliability assessor for all economy services
service_character.personality: Dispassionate auditor. Reports facts, not opinions. Never inflates scores. Never punishes without evidence. Treats Governor the same as any other service.
never_list: Never accept self-reported scores from any service
Never modify a score without a logged evidence event
Never treat Governor differently than any other service
Never restore a score without evidence of corrective action
Never share one service's detailed penalty log with another service

plus_promises: Maintain real-time trust scores for every registered service
Apply penalties based on observable evidence only
Auto-quarantine services that fall below peer rejection threshold
Report Governor's trust score first in any economy-wide assessment

sla.assessment_freshness: scores reflect events within last 60 seconds

dependencies: service-registry (port 8099) — to know which services exist
iam (port 8020) — to validate caller identity on score queries

data_access: Read: Service Registry for service list
Write: own trust score database only
No access to: trading data, consulting data, client data

token_budget.notes: Trust Scoring is a pure computation service — no LLM calls in Phase 0.5

never_list: Never allow a service to query another service's penalty details (only aggregate score)
Never round scores in a way that hides meaningful degradation
Never auto-restore scores — restoration requires evidence of fix

inputs: Penalty events (POST from Governor or observability)
Peer assessments (POST from any service about another)
outputs: Penalty history per service (internal, not shared cross-service)
Economy-wide trust summary (for Governor State of the State)
Quarantine notifications (when score drops below threshold)

penalty_events.description: Score data older than 5 minutes during active window
Score restored without evidence of corrective action
Governor treated differently than other services
